description: Vision-derived content tags per local image, keyed by <GalleryName>/<filename> (stable local-content identity, independent of Milovana IDs). Used to select images matching a script's subject + pace + exposure. explicitness and pace are TWO INDEPENDENT AXES: explicitness = how much is shown (ordered: clothed < underwear < partial-nudity < nude < explicit); pace = how energetic/fast the scene is (1-5, ignoring nudity). They do not combine into one score (e.g. a nude/pace-1 pose is more exposed but calmer than a clothed/pace-2 shot). explicitness guide: clothed = dressed, nothing bared; underwear = bra/panties/thong/lingerie as worn; partial-nudity = one region bared (breasts OR bottom); nude = fully exposed/undressed; explicit = explicit sexual act. Tags are Claude's visual judgement and should be user-verified. Join with asset-map.json on the filename key to get the playable locator. See EOS-Tease-Authoring-Guide.md §5.4.

subject: solo
machine
partner
vocabulary.pace: Energy/tempo of the scene only, independent of explicitness. 1 (still/sensual/posed) to 5 (frantic/hard). Anchored to BPM bands for matching to the metronome (1: <=40, 2: 40-70, 3: 70-110, 4: 110-150, 5: 150+).

LeahGotti_001.themeNote: NOT a clean bucket: opens with two solo sensual shots then escalates to explicit partner sex. Illustrates why a real tease should split these into separate themed galleries (the partner shots are unusable anywhere a 'model alone' constraint applies, e.g. a tutorial).

LeahGotti_002.themeNote: Clean solo set throughout (model alone, warm indoor villa, white tank-top striptease). Tutorial-safe under a 'model alone / no partner' constraint. Note exposure climbs across the set (clothed -> underwear -> partial-nudity -> nude) while pace stays low (1-2) - a posed striptease is calm regardless of nudity, which is exactly why the two axes are kept separate.
